title: Contacts
lede: Contacts are the people you do business with — usually customers converted from leads. This guide shows you how to add, edit, filter, and tag them.

steps.title: Open the Contacts list
steps.content:
1. In the left navigation, open CRM and click Contacts (or go to /crm/contacts).
  2. The table shows each contact's Name, Email, Phone, Stage, and Account (their company).
  3. Click a name or avatar to open the full profile with call and message history.

A contact is a real person in your book of business. The companies they belong to are stored as Accounts.

images.caption: The Contacts list with search, filters, and tags
The Contacts list with search, filters, and tags

steps.title: Add a new contact
steps.content:
1. Click Create (New Contact) at the top of the list.
  2. Enter the First Name and Last Name (both required).
  3. Add addresses in the Emails field — the first one becomes the primary email.
  4. Click Add Phone Number to add a number and pick a label (Mobile, Work, Home, etc.).
  5. Optionally choose an Account, set a Stage, and fill in the mailing address.
  6. Click Save.

steps.title: Edit a contact
steps.content:
1. Click a contact's name to open their profile.
  2. Update any field — add more emails or phone numbers, change the Stage, or link a different Account.
  3. Remember: the primary email is always the first one in the Emails list, so put it first if you need to change which email is primary.
  4. Save your changes.

steps.title: Search, filter, and tag
steps.content:
• Use the search box and switch between searching by Name, Email, or Phone.
  • Add filters for Stage, Tags, Address, City, or Owner.
  • Add tags on a contact to group people (for example VIP or renewal), then filter the list by that tag.

steps.title: Bulk actions and cleanup
steps.content:
1. Tick the checkboxes to select contacts (or select all that match your filters).
  2. Use the bulk toolbar to Delete, Assign to an Account, or Change Owner.
  3. Run Find Duplicates by phone or email to remove repeated records and keep your list clean.

metaDescription: Add, edit, filter, and tag customer contacts in the iMash CRM. Store multiple emails and phone numbers, link accounts, and clean up duplicates.
metaKeywords: contacts, CRM contacts, customer records, contact management, multiple emails, phone numbers, contact tags
